question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be between 78-79°F on March 21?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 21 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ga/atlanta/KATL.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors 84-85°F (47.5%) and 82-83°F (30.5%) as Atlanta's highest temperature on March 21, driven by the latest NOAA and ECMWF model ensembles projecting peak heating in that range under a dominant upper-level ridge over the Southeast. This pattern promotes subsidence warming, clear skies, and southerly winds advecting Gulf moisture, with NWS Atlanta's official forecast calling for a high near 84°F amid light winds and ample sunshine. Recent 00Z GFS and Euro runs slightly elevated mid-80s probabilities by refining boundary layer mixing, countering minor cloud threats. Climatologically, March averages 66°F highs but sees warm anomalies like this 20-25% of the time per NOAA records, though ensemble spreads highlight 78-88°F uncertainty ahead of final 12Z updates.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Miami high temperature of 78-79°F at 42.5% implied probability, driven by the latest NOAA and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on mild conditions from a lingering cool front and neutral ENSO patterns suppressing extremes. Supporting this, GFS model runs updated March 19 project peaks of 78-80°F amid light southerly winds and sea surface temperatures around 78°F, aligning with March climatology where daily highs average 79°F from 1991-2020 normals. Lower odds for 80-81°F (28%) reflect slight warm-up potential from diurnal heating, while 76-77°F (15%) accounts for cloud cover variability; rare outliers like 86°F+ (0.3%) ignore historical precedents of sub-85°F March peaks 95% of the time. Key watch: NWS Miami's 18Z update today could shift odds if upper-level trough deepens.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 21 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Miami high temperature of 78-79°F at 42.5% implied probability, driven by the latest NOAA and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on mild conditions from a lingering cool front and neutral ENSO patterns suppressing extremes. Supporting this, GFS model runs updated March 19 project peaks of 78-80°F amid light southerly winds and sea surface temperatures around 78°F, aligning with March climatology where daily highs average 79°F from 1991-2020 normals. Lower odds for 80-81°F (28%) reflect slight warm-up potential from diurnal heating, while 76-77°F (15%) accounts for cloud cover variability; rare outliers like 86°F+ (0.3%) ignore historical precedents of sub-85°F March peaks 95% of the time. Key watch: NWS Miami's 18Z update today could shift odds if upper-level trough deepens.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors 72-73°F (31.3% implied probability) as Chicago's highest temperature on March 21, propelled by the latest NOAA GFS and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on peaks of 72-74°F amid a potent upper-level ridge ushering southerly winds and Gulf-sourced warmth. Differentiating this narrow lead from 70-71°F (27%) hinges on model divergence in afternoon cloud cover and boundary layer mixing, with clearer skies potentially boosting peaks by 1-2°F via enhanced insolation on preconditioned mild soils. Historical March baselines hover near 45°F, rendering these odds a stark anomaly driven by persistent Arctic Oscillation negativity, though late tweaks in jet stream phasing could shave outcomes lower.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 21 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble weather model forecasts from ECMWF and GFS, updated in recent runs, project Ankara's highest temperature on March 21 at around 11°C, driving the market's 47% implied probability for that outcome amid a mild low-pressure system capping highs. Turkish State Meteorological Service data reinforces this, with March climatological normals averaging 12°C but recent upper-air cooling trends—evident in soundings showing stable inversions—shifting odds downward from 12°C (14.5%) and higher (11.8%) while boosting 10°C (23.5%). Historical observations rarely dip below 9°C this date, aligning low probabilities for colder outcomes; traders await hourly station reports from Esenboğa Airport for final resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a highest temperature of 17°C in Wellington on March 21, driven by MetService New Zealand's latest forecast models projecting a daytime maximum of 17°C under partly cloudy skies with moderate southerly winds limiting warming. This aligns with ensemble predictions from global models like ECMWF and GFS, which show consistent cooling influences from a post-frontal airmass following recent rain. Historical March data from NIWA indicates average highs around 19°C, but current upper-air patterns favor below-normal temperatures, supporting the 92.5% implied probability. Realistic challenges include an unforeseen northerly wind shift injecting warmer air or shortwave model errors, potentially pushing toward 18–19°C, though low-level shear reduces such risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast drives trader consensus toward a 24°C high on March 21 at 54% implied probability, projecting mainly fine conditions with light winds and temperatures peaking near 24-25°C amid a stable high-pressure ridge over southern China. This aligns with March's climatological norms, where daily highs average 23°C, influenced by subtropical maritime air masses moderating extremes. Recent observations show 20 March reaching 23.2°C, reinforcing mild trends without heat advection, while ensemble models from ECMWF and GFS converge on 23-25°C, sidelining hotter outliers above 26°C (6% combined). Uncertainty lingers from potential afternoon sea breezes, but current data favors the 23-25°C cluster holding 98% market odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors a highest temperature of 15°C in Shanghai on March 21 (62.5% implied probability), propelled by the latest ECMWF and GFS model ensembles converging on daytime highs near 15°C amid mild southerly winds displacing recent cold continental air masses. Shanghai's March climatology typically sees average highs of 13-15°C, amplified slightly by the city's urban heat island effect, with recent observations showing overnight lows stabilizing around 8-10°C after a brief cool spell earlier in the week. Secondary odds for 14°C (17.9%) and 16°C (17.0%) reflect ensemble spreads capturing variability from sea breeze influences and jet stream positioning, though official China Meteorological Administration updates reinforce the 15°C peak as most probable barring unforeseen frontal passages.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 21 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors a highest temperature of 15°C in Shanghai on March 21 (62.5% implied probability), propelled by the latest ECMWF and GFS model ensembles converging on daytime highs near 15°C amid mild southerly winds displacing recent cold continental air masses. Shanghai's March climatology typically sees average highs of 13-15°C, amplified slightly by the city's urban heat island effect, with recent observations showing overnight lows stabilizing around 8-10°C after a brief cool spell earlier in the week. Secondary odds for 14°C (17.9%) and 16°C (17.0%) reflect ensemble spreads capturing variability from sea breeze influences and jet stream positioning, though official China Meteorological Administration updates reinforce the 15°C peak as most probable barring unforeseen frontal passages.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a 33°C high in Singapore on March 21 (63% implied probability), driven by the National Environment Agency's (NEA) latest forecast predicting thundery showers after noon with maximum temperatures reaching 33°C amid persistent inter-monsoon heat. March climatology supports this, with historical averages around 32-33°C during the dry transition from the northeast monsoon, amplified by urban heat island effects and El Niño residuals boosting regional warmth. Recent observations show March 20 peaking at 33.2°C, while model ensembles from ECMWF and GFS converge on 32-34°C peaks before potential cloud cover caps extremes, explaining the 24.5% odds for 32°C and 10% for 34°C as traders hedge forecast uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts, updated within the last 24 hours, project Milan's highest temperature on March 21 at 15-16°C, driving trader consensus toward 16°C or higher at 53% and exactly 15°C at 37%. A persistent high-pressure ridge over Western Europe is channeling mild southerly airflow into northern Italy, elevating temperatures above the historical March 21 average of 12-13°C observed at Milan Linate Airport. Yesterday's observed high of 14°C aligns with this warming trend, though model spread introduces ±2°C uncertainty ahead of final NWP runs. Low probabilities for sub-14°C reflect minimal risk of cooler Atlantic intrusions per current synoptic patterns.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Malpensa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/it/milan/LIMC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts, updated within the last 24 hours, project Milan's highest temperature on March 21 at 15-16°C, driving trader consensus toward 16°C or higher at 53% and exactly 15°C at 37%. A persistent high-pressure ridge over Western Europe is channeling mild southerly airflow into northern Italy, elevating temperatures above the historical March 21 average of 12-13°C observed at Milan Linate Airport. Yesterday's observed high of 14°C aligns with this warming trend, though model spread introduces ±2°C uncertainty ahead of final NWP runs. Low probabilities for sub-14°C reflect minimal risk of cooler Atlantic intrusions per current synoptic patterns.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a high of 13°C in Madrid on March 21 (37% implied probability), driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS model ensembles converging on mild northerly winds and persistent cloud cover capping daytime heating under a blocking high-pressure pattern. AEMET's official forecast aligns, projecting a maximum near 13°C amid below-average March conditions, following recent cold air advection that kept early-week highs in the 10-12°C range. Historical data shows Madrid's March highs averaging 16°C but varying ±4°C due to Atlantic influences; no heat signals from sea surface temperatures or jet stream shifts support outliers like 15°C+ (under 10% combined odds), with traders eyeing tomorrow's 12Z model update for potential tweaks.

Upcoming hourly updates from CWA could shift odds if cloud cover increases.
